---
title: "master_thesis_abiotic"
author: "Savannah K. Miller"
date: "2026-06-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
```


```{r}


library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)

cdo_token <- "gbomlSahRoNqHntPqhNYszYpaSkhufSr"
api_endpoint <- "https://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/cdo-web/api/v2/data"

resp <- GET(api_endpoint,
  add_headers(token = cdo_token),
  query = list(
    datasetid = "GHCND",
    stationid = "GHCND:USW00023272",
    startdate = "2022-12-25",
    enddate = "2023-01-05",
    datatypeid = "PRCP",
    units = "standard",
    limit = 1000
  ))

status_code(resp)
content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")

station_check <- GET("https://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/cdo-web/api/v2/stations/GHCND:USC00047740",
  add_headers(token = cdo_token))

status_code(station_check)
content(station_check, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")
```

```{r}
precip_2022 <- fromJSON(content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))$results %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    value = as.numeric(value)
  ) %>%
  select(date, value)

precip_2022

```


This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r, 2026 data}
resp_2026 <- GET(api_endpoint,
  add_headers(token = cdo_token),
  query = list(
    datasetid = "GHCND",
    stationid = "GHCND:USW00023272",
    startdate = "2025-12-30",
    enddate = "2026-01-10",
    datatypeid = "PRCP",
    units = "standard",
    limit = 1000
  ))

status_code(resp_2026)
precip_2026 <- fromJSON(content(resp_2026, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))$results %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    value = as.numeric(value)
  ) %>%
  select(date, value)

precip_2026
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r, plotting 2022 and 2026 rain data}

library(ggplot2)

precip_2022$event <- "Dec 2022 / Jan 2023"
precip_2026$event <- "Jan 2026"

combined_precip <- bind_rows(precip_2022, precip_2026) %>%
  group_by(event) %>%
  mutate(day_num = row_number()) %>%  # aligns both events on a comparable x-axis
  ungroup()

ggplot(combined_precip, aes(x = day_num, y = value, fill = event)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Precipitation: Dec 2022/Jan 2023 vs Jan 2026",
    x = "Day of Event Window",
    y = "Precipitation (inches)",
    fill = "Event"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r, 2022 NOAA monthly summary pull}

station_gsom_check <- GET("https://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/cdo-web/api/v2/stations/GHCND:USW00023272",
  add_headers(token = cdo_token),
  query = list(datasetid = "GSOM"))

status_code(station_gsom_check)
content(station_gsom_check, "text", encoding = "UTF-8")

# Pull a full year of daily data (we know this works)
precip_full2022 <- get_noaa_precip("2022-07-01", "2023-06-30", "GHCND:USW00023272", cdo_token)

# Aggregate to monthly totals yourself
library(lubridate)

monthly_data2022 <- precip_full2022 %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(monthly_total_in = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

monthly_data

plot_2022 <- ggplot(monthly_data2022, aes(x = month, y = monthly_total_in)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_date(
    date_labels = "%b %Y",
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    limits = as.Date(c("2022-07-01", "2023-06-30"))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Precipitation: July 2022 – June 2023",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Total Precipitation (inches)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave("monthly_precip_2022_2023.png", plot = plot_2022, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}

today <- Sys.Date()
end_date <- min(as.Date("2026-06-30"), today)

precip_full2026 <- get_noaa_precip("2025-07-01", as.character(end_date), "GHCND:USW00023272", cdo_token)

# Sanity check on the RIGHT object
precip_full2026 %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  count(month)

# Actually build monthly_data2026 from precip_full2026 — this step was missing
monthly_data2026 <- precip_full2026 %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(monthly_total_in = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(complete = ifelse(month == floor_date(today, "month"), "Partial month", "Complete"))

monthly_data2026   # print and visually confirm before plotting

plot_2026 <- ggplot(monthly_data2026, aes(x = month, y = monthly_total_in, fill = complete)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Complete" = "steelblue", "Partial month" = "gray70")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(title = "Monthly Precipitation: July 2025 – June 2026", x = "Month", y = "Inches", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave("monthly_precip_2025_2026.png", plot = plot_2026, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}

# Save as CSV (most portable, easy to reopen in R or Excel)
write.csv(precip_full2022, "precip_daily_2022_2023.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(precip_full2026, "precip_daily_2025_2026.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(monthly_data2022, "precip_monthly_2022_2023.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(monthly_data2026, "precip_monthly_2025_2026.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```


```{r, let's try something (revisit this)}

# You need a date range or representative date for each Study.Phase
# Example structure - adjust to your actual phase-to-date mapping

phase_dates <- cm_data %>%
  group_by(Study.Phase) %>%
  summarise(Study.Phase = min("M1"), end_date = max("M10"), .groups = "drop")

# Sum precipitation within each phase's date window
precip_by_phase <- phase_dates %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    total_precip = sum(precip_full2022$value[precip_full2022$date >= M1 & 
                                                precip_full2022$date <= end_date], na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Join precip onto your flux summary
time_dist_summary <- time_dist_summary %>%
  left_join(precip_by_phase %>% select(Study.Phase, total_precip), by = "Study.Phase")



ggplot(time_dist_summary, aes(x = Study.Phase)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = total_precip), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = mean_flux, group = 1), method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = mean_flux), color = "darkred", size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dist, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Precipitation (in)",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ ., name = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3)))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(title = paste("Flux & Precipitation Through Time by Distance at", "Corte Madera"), x = "Study Phase")

```



```{r, tides}
library(purrr)

get_noaa_water_level <- function(start_date, end_date, station = "9414290") {
  api_endpoint <- "https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/api/prod/datagetter"
  resp <- GET(api_endpoint, query = list(
    begin_date = start_date,
    end_date = end_date,
    station = station,
    product = "water_level",
    datum = "MLLW",
    units = "english",
    time_zone = "lst_ldt",
    format = "json"
  ))
  fromJSON(content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))$data %>%
    mutate(
      t = as.POSIXct(t, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M", tz = "America/Los_Angeles"),
      v = as.numeric(v)
    ) %>%
    rename(datetime = t, water_level_ft = v) %>%
    select(datetime, water_level_ft)
}

# Build monthly date chunks for July 2022 - June 2023
month_starts <- seq(as.Date("2022-07-01"), as.Date("2023-06-01"), by = "month")
month_ends <- ceiling_date(month_starts, "month") - 1

# Pull each month and stack them
tide_full2022 <- map2_dfr(month_starts, month_ends, function(start, end) {
  get_noaa_water_level(format(start, "%Y%m%d"), format(end, "%Y%m%d"))
})
```
```{r}

monthly_tide2022 <- tide_full2022 %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(as.Date(datetime), "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    monthly_max_ft = max(water_level_ft, na.rm = TRUE),
    monthly_mean_ft = mean(water_level_ft, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}

ggplot(monthly_tide2022, aes(x = month, y = monthly_max_ft)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkcyan") +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Maximum Water Level: July 2022 – June 2023",
    x = "Month", y = "Max Water Level (ft, MLLW)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
